title: Psalms 116
subtitle: I Love the LORD, for He Heard My Voice

sections.header: Verses 1–9 — “The LORD Is Gracious and Righteous”

heb.paragraph: "I love (ʾāhabtî) the LORD, for he heard my voice" (v.1). Personal testimony: death’s cords entangled me (v.3), I called on the LORD (v.4), he saved me (v.6). "The LORD is gracious and righteous; our God is full of compassion" (v.5). "Return to your rest, my soul, for the LORD has been good to you" (v.7). The soul that was troubled is counselled: return to rest.

notes.note: "I love the LORD, for he heard my voice; he heard my cry for mercy. Because he turned his ear to me, I will call on him as long as I live." Love born from being heard. The response to divine attention is lifelong prayer.
"The cords of death entangled me. Then I called on the name of the LORD. The LORD is gracious and righteous. When I was brought low, he saved me." Death’s proximity, God’s rescue, character revealed.

sections.header: Verses 10–19 — “Precious in the Sight of the LORD Is the Death of His Faithful Servants”

heb.paragraph: "Precious (yāqār) in the sight of the LORD is the death of his faithful servants" (v.15). The most-quoted funeral verse. God does not view the death of his faithful casually — it is costly, significant, weighty to him. "What shall I return to the LORD for all his goodness to me? I will lift up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD" (vv.12–13). Paul quotes v.10 in 2 Corinthians 4:13: "I believed; therefore I have spoken."

notes.note: "What shall I return to the LORD? I will lift up the cup of salvation." The gratitude question: how do I repay God? The answer: receive more. Lift the cup — accept salvation again.
"Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his faithful servants." God does not treat his servants’ deaths lightly. Each one is precious — costly, noticed, mourned.

"I will sacrifice a thank offering to you and call on the name of the LORD, in the courts of the house of the LORD — in your midst, Jerusalem." Public worship: the courts, the city, the community. Testimony is corporate.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: " Death’s proximity, God’s rescue, character revealed…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVThen I called on the name of the Lord: “Lord, save me!”ESVThen I called on the name of the LORD: \u201cO LORD, I pray, deliver my soul!\u201dNIVReturn to your rest, my soul, for the Lord has been good to you.ESVReturn, O my soul, to your rest; for the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.

rec.text: ""I love the LORD, for he heard my voice; he heard my cry for mercy. Because he turned his ear to me, I will call on him as long as I live." Love born from being heard. The response to divine attention…"

rows.label: Heard and Saved — 116:1–9
rows.text: "I LOVE the LORD, for he heard my voice." Death’s cords. "Return to your rest, my soul."

hebtext.note: "I love (ʾāhabtî) the LORD, for he heard my voice" (v.1). Personal testimony: death’s cords entangled me (v.3), I called on the LORD (v.4), he saved me (v.6). "The LORD is gracious and righteous; our God is full of compassion" (v.5). "Return to your rest, my soul, for the LORD has been good to you" (v.7). The soul that was troubled is counselled: return to rest.

"Precious (yāqār) in the sight of the LORD is the death of his faithful servants" (v.15). The most-quoted funeral verse. God does not view the death of his faithful casually — it is costly, significant, weighty to him. "What shall I return to the LORD for all his goodness to me? I will lift up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD" (vv.12–13). Paul quotes v.10 in 2 Corinthians 4:13: "I believed; therefore I have spoken."

themes.note: Mercy and Worship peak: rescued from death, cup of salvation lifted, precious death. Paul quotes v.10 (2 Cor 4:13).
